title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return for the Celtics' Saturday game against the Wizards after resting his right Achilles injury.
Tatum is on a minutes restriction after his first three games back from a right ruptured Achilles suffered 10 months ago.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable for Saturday's game; Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson is now available on Netflix, focusing on a soldier's PTSD struggles.
'Zootopia 2' streams on Disney+, featuring Nick and Judy tackling a new mystery in the animal city.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' stars Steve Carell as an author navigating college life after personal upheaval.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots sign former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen their offensive line after allowing 68 sacks last season.
Vera-Tucker has started 43 NFL games but missed last season due to a triceps injury, sparking health concerns.
Morgan Moses played a key role in convincing Vera-Tucker, his former Jets teammate, to join the Patriots.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders for the 2026 season.
Newcomer Caleb Durbin likely to start as Boston's third baseman, with bullpen strengthened by Danny Coulombe.
Crochet praised for his performance, leading MLB in strikeouts and drawing comparisons to historic pitchers.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC makes its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium, marking a new era since 2018.
Head coach Filipa Patão plans to implement an attacking 4-4-2 formation, adapting to NWSL's roster constraints.
Gillette Stadium's new grass field is set to debut, potentially benefiting Boston's possession-focused playing style.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating ending the biannual clock change, proposing permanent time legislation through bill S.2157.
Senate President Karen Spilka supports the bill, citing disruptions to routines and health linked to time changes.
Research indicates clock changes can increase heart attacks and strokes; permanent time may improve public health and productivity.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People plans 2026 Pride celebration on June 6, featuring a parade, festival, and various events.
The parade will start at 11 a.m. at Copley Square and conclude at Boston Common with concerts and a festival.
Mayor Michelle Wu supports the event, endorsing its mission of celebrating diversity and intersectionality in the LGBTQ+ community.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released and reunited with family in Massachusetts.
Federal agents claimed the girl was "rescued" from gang members, while officials suggest she was used to locate her father.
The girl now faces immigration court, and her lawyer plans to petition for her legal residency to remain with her aunt.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are experiencing a scoring slump, jeopardizing their playoff position as they hold a single-point lead for the final wild-card spot.
David Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm struggle in 5-on-5 play, prompting discussions of a lineup reshuffle to boost scoring.
Coach Marco Sturm considers integrating prospect James Hagens and adjusting forward lines as Bruins face a critical road trip.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss researcher at Harvard Medical School, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for trauma research funding.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for Brigham and Women’s Hospital’s Stepping Strong Center through his marathon participation.
His year in Boston focuses on neurorehabilitation, inspired by trauma-associated challenges and underfunding in trauma research programs.
